explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: Documentation addresses legal, ethical, and liability concerns related to AI. It also addresses concerns regarding data sourcing and disclosure.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This white paper by Berkley Design Professional provides evidence for **governance**, **oversight**, and **external accountability**. It highlights the need for firms to understand AI risks, establish strategies, and manage implementation, underscoring the importance of governance through AI policies, leadership identification, and defined use cases. The document also emphasizes human oversight and responsible control, directly supporting the oversight pillar, and addresses legal, ethical, and liability concerns, including data sourcing and disclosure, which speaks to external accountability.
relevant_sources.title: Berkley Design Professional: AI in Design and Construction White Paper

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Documentation discusses establishing an AI policy, identifying leadership, and forming a team as components of a governance structure. It also highlights the need for firms to understand AI risks, establish strategies, manage implementation, and reference defined AI use cases.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This white paper by Berkley Service Professional provides evidence for the **governance** and **oversight** pillars of responsible AI. It discusses establishing an AI policy, identifying leadership, and forming a team, which are key components of a governance structure. Furthermore, the paper strongly emphasizes the critical need for human oversight, expert review of AI output, and caution against over-reliance, directly supporting the oversight pillar.
relevant_sources.title: Berkley SP White Paper: Artificial Intelligence for Insurance Agencies and Brokers

relevant_sources.summary: This white paper by Berkley Design Professional provides evidence for **governance**, **oversight**, and **external accountability**. It highlights the need for firms to understand AI risks, establish strategies, and manage implementation, underscoring the importance of governance through AI policies, leadership identification, and defined use cases. The document also emphasizes human oversight and responsible control, directly supporting the oversight pillar, and addresses legal, ethical, and liability concerns, including data sourcing and disclosure, which speaks to external accountability.
relevant_sources.title: Berkley Design Professional: AI in Design and Construction White Paper

oversight.findings: Documentation emphasizes the critical need for human oversight and responsible control of AI. It also describes the need for expert review of AI output and references caution against over-reliance on AI.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This white paper by Berkley Service Professional provides evidence for the **governance** and **oversight** pillars of responsible AI. It discusses establishing an AI policy, identifying leadership, and forming a team, which are key components of a governance structure. Furthermore, the paper strongly emphasizes the critical need for human oversight, expert review of AI output, and caution against over-reliance, directly supporting the oversight pillar.

relevant_sources.summary: This white paper by Berkley Design Professional provides evidence for **governance**, **oversight**, and **external accountability**. It highlights the need for firms to understand AI risks, establish strategies, and manage implementation, underscoring the importance of governance through AI policies, leadership identification, and defined use cases. The document also emphasizes human oversight and responsible control, directly supporting the oversight pillar, and addresses legal, ethical, and liability concerns, including data sourcing and disclosure, which speaks to external accountability.

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: W.R. Berkley's published materials emphasize the critical need for human oversight in AI systems, with disclosures also describing the need for expert review of AI output. Furthermore, the materials discuss establishing an AI policy and identifying AI leadership within its governance framework. Oversight, governance, and external accountability are addressed at the policy level, accounting for 3 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Drawing from 12 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for transparency, fairness, and 2 additional pillars.
